name: Peñas de Herrera - circular from Talamantes
name_original: Peñas de Herrera - circular desde Talamantes
region_intro: Hiking around Talamantes offers diverse landscapes near the Moncayo Massif, a prominent natural feature. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including mountain ascents and natural formations. Opportunities for exploration include areas around Pico Moncayo and sites like Pozo de los Aines. This area provides a range of hiking experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Talamantes The most popular hiking route is Peñas de Herrera - circular from Talamantes, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding Moncayo landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cueva de los Pilares and Cerro Morrón Loop, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.7 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain and leads through rugged terrain. Local hikers also love the Cuevas de Añón loop from Alcalá de Moncayo, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through areas near natural caves, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Talamantes is defined by the Moncayo Massif, natural caves, and diverse landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Talamantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Talamantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Talamantes?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes in the Talamantes area, offering a variety of experiences. These include 1 easy, 21 moderate, and 4 difficult trails, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking near Talamantes?
A: The terrain around Talamantes is diverse, influenced by the nearby Moncayo Massif. You can expect varied landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to rugged paths and areas near natural caves. Trails often feature significant elevation changes, providing panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Talamantes?
A: Yes, Talamantes offers several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971963175/penas-de-herrera-circular-from-talamantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de Herrera - circular from Talamantes&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 15.1 km trail. Another is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971994351/cueva-de-los-pilares-and-cerro-morron-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares and Cerro Morrón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 15.7 km with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see on the trails?
A: Hiking around Talamantes allows you to explore several natural features and landmarks. You can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, or reach the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Morrón Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Peñas de Herrera&lt;/a&gt; also offer scenic highlights.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy hiking route available. For a moderate option that is not overly strenuous, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17544360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de Añón loop from Alcalá de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.8 km long and takes about 2 hours 24 minutes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Talamantes?
A: The hiking trails in Talamantes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 stars from nearly 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes and the opportunities for exploring natural formations and mountain scenery.

Q: Is Talamantes a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Talamantes offers a range of trails, including some that are less challenging. For families, focusing on the easier or shorter moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17544360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de Añón loop from Alcalá de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, would be most suitable. These trails allow for exploration without excessive difficulty.

Q: Are there any trails that explore natural caves?
A: Yes, the region is known for its natural caves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17544360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de Añón loop from Alcalá de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24312335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Añón de Moncayo – Cuevas de Añón loop from Añón de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; both lead through areas near natural caves, offering a unique element to your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Talamantes?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the diverse terrain and mountain features suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery for hiking. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter might bring colder conditions and potential snow in higher areas like the Moncayo Massif.

Q: Are there any trails that feature gorges or water elements?
A: Yes, you can find trails that include gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Boticario Spring – Valdeherrera Gorge loop from Talamantes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.2 km route that explores the Valdeherrera Gorge. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the area.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Talamantes?
A: Specific parking locations for each trail are not detailed in the guide. However, as Talamantes is a rural area, it's generally advisable to look for designated parking areas near trailheads or in the village itself. Always ensure you park responsibly and respect local regulations.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in Talamantes?
A: Hiking durations in Talamantes vary significantly based on the trail's length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17544360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de Añón loop from Alcalá de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; takes about 2 hours 24 minutes, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971994351/cueva-de-los-pilares-and-cerro-morron-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares and Cerro Morrón Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take around 5 hours 28 minutes.
tips: The Talamantes castle was built in the 11th century with the purpose of defending the border crossing between Castile and Aragon in the Valdeherrera and Valdetreviño ravines. Although much of the fortification is in a state of ruin, the north wall and one of its towers are still quite well preserved.
Beautiful trail that runs through the top of the Sierra de Moncayo with magnificent views of the Peñas de Herrera, Cerro Morrón and surrounding mountains.

The Valdeherrera ravine connects the town of Talamantes with the Estacas hill, a point where the GR 90 forks into two variants. The ravine, although it consists of steep slopes, offers fantastic views of the surrounding rocks.

waypoints.name: View of Peñas de Herrera from the Sierra de Moncayo
